ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FIRST ACCOUNTING OF TRUSTEE
Off Calendar Aug-22-2016 Continued To Sep-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Franchesca Callejo for (successor trustee) Barbara McWhirter who appeared via court call. Paul Melbostad (previous counsel) for Barbara McWhirter. Amy Harrington (prior counsel for former conservator/trustee) Franza Giffen. Court appointed counsel Caroline Hinshaw (via court call) for Ms. Kelly-Ryder.

Ms. Callejo stated that there are two issues, payments to counsel including Ms. Harrington, Ms. Giffen, Mr. Hartog, the petition in the conservatorship for fees to Mr. Melbostad and that Ms. Callejo has not had a chance to review the petition. The Court asked that the fee issue be addressed first. Ms. Callejo reported that she is new to the case, that she relied on Ms. McWhirter to look at the accounting, that subsequently the petition was filed in the conservatorship case, that they have concerns regarding duplication, that Mr. Melbostad's paralegal prepared the accounting at the rate of $160.00 per hour, that it could have been done at a lower rate, that Ms. McWhirter says that it took forever even though Ms. McWhirter provided the information to the firm, that Ms. Callejo would like additional time to review the accounting, that they think it should be consolidated with the conservatorship matter set for Wednesday, that they would like to be able to itemize the issues that they have with Mr. Melbostad's fee requests and allow time for Mr. Melbostad to respond. Ms. Harrington addressed the interest request citing 1000 & 685.010 stating that she is entitled to interest, that the services were provided in 2012 but not paid until 2014, that it was a very contentious case, that they received intimidating letters from Mr. Hartog and that if she is not entitled to interest would request that the Court provide explanation as to why not. The Court asked Ms. Harrington if there have been fee requests in excess of what was awarded and Ms. Harrington responded no. Ms. Callejo stated that Mr. Hartog's office billed for fees, that there was not enough money at the time of his request to pay him, that Ms. McWhirter made payments and ended up overpaying in the amount of $1100.00 and that Mr. Hartog did not let them know that there was a limit on the award of fees. Mr. Melbostad stated that the fees were paid out of escrow and that Ms. McWhirter had not asked for his advice. Ms. Hinshaw stated her agreement that fees were paid out of escrow. Mr. Melbostad stated that Ms. Callejo's proposal makes sense, that they are talking about a 2 year 11 month period, that when this began Ms. McWhirter was being besieged by attorneys and nursing facilities, that he knew that he would not get paid until the Divisidero property was sold, that his firm provided real estate and landlord tenant issue help and that they do not want to be paid what they are not entitled to. Ms. Callejo stated that the Court need to make a decision regarding 685.010 and that there is no agreement regarding it amongst counsel. Ms. Harrington stated that if the court makes a ruling that it does not apply they would like to file a reply, that in regards to Mr. Hartog he took a check from Ms. Kelly-Ryder after she had been declared incapacitated and that it overdrew her account. Ms. Callejo stated that she has sent a letter to Mr. Hartog regarding the overpayment, that she has not had a response as of yet and that she will file a status as to the overpayment. The accounting petition was continued to September 19, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and the court is hopeful that the discussions counsel have will narrow the issues. The Petition for order fixing, allowing and directing payment by trustee is case PCN-13-296492 Conservatorship of Joan Kelley-Ryder is continued to the same date. Ms. Callejo stated that she will convey their concerns to Mr. Melbostad informally and that she will file objections by September 2, 2016.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PTR-13-296491 The Joan Kelly-Ryder Trust and PCN-13-296492 Conservatorship of Joan Kelly-Ryder.
Appearances: Paul Melbostad for and with (petitioner/trustee) Barbara McWhirter. Court appointed counsel Caroline Hinshaw for Joan Kelley-Ryder. David Gellman, estate planning attorney for Joan Kelly-Ryder.

The Court asked how much time was needed for the accounting in the trust matter and Mr. Melbostad asked for a 30 day continuance. Mr. Melbostad addressed the petition in the conservatorship stating that it is for substituted judgment, that they have complied, that they filed a detailed petition stating what the conservatee would have done, that it is consistent with her past practices, that subsequent changes were inconsistent with all previous documents and practices, that it left out family members when there had been no issues brought up and cited 14.4(m) regarding the trustee being subject to the same terms and conditions. Mr. Gellman stated that everything was in his declaration, that he knew Ms. Kelly-Ryder well, that she was clear in regards to her donative intent and that it was very clear in the last year that she knew she could not trust her own judgment. Ms. Hinshaw stated that she has looked at the statutes and agrees with Mr. Melbostad, that she has always had concerns regarding the estate plan and that it was confusing. The Court stated that it feels the appropriate path is to amend and asked if there was any other basis to show it should not be done. Mr. Melbostad stated that Ms. McWhirter just reminded him that an evaluation had been done on Ms. Kelly-Ryder and that she no longer has capacity. Mr. Gellman stated that it needs to be done at some point and that it seems safest to do it now. The Court stated that it is the mechanism not the timing that is at issue, why not amend to modify or revoke and that it makes a difference regarding the request for relief. Mr. Melbostad stated that that is why they put so much into the petition. Ms. Hinshaw stated that in regards to amending the code section says that it is not exclusive. The Status on filing a first account (PTR-13-296491) was continued to December 21, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The petition for substituted judgment, etc., (PCN-13-296492) was continued to December 21, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and counsel was told that if the court does not change its line of analysis the matter will be taken under submission.

Mr. Gothelf stated that the properties are selling in excess of $3,000,000.00 or higher, that the property requires extensive renovation and dealing with the City Planner, that there is an issue of a tenant occupying the building and that it requires due diligence from the buyer. Mr. Gothelf also stated that they received an offer of 4.7 million, that buyers are reluctant to get involved with Court sales and that the property was marketed. Ms. Hinshaw expressed concern about the impact the sale will have on her Client and her care. Mr. Melbostad stated that he was not aware Ms. McWirther was proceeding without Court approval, that this occurred during a time when he was unavailable, raised the issue of capital gains and asked to be allowed to petition ex parte for a 1031 exchange for an apartment building/property located near Santa Barbara. Ms. McWhirther asked for 45 days to identify the properties for the 1031 exchange and 190 days to close escrow. The Court granted the Petition for Authority to Sell Trustor's Residence and to Enter Into Exclusive Listing Agreement but will require Court confirmation of the sale (subject to review and modification of the order). The Court informed Mr. Melbostad that the 1031 exchange needs to be brought by way a full petition and that they can ask to shorten time, and stated that Ms. Hinshaw needs to be involved and in communication with regard to the 1031 exchange issue.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-31-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following cases were heard: PTR-13-296491 The Joan Kelley-Ryder Trust and PCN-13-296492 Conservatorship of Joan Kelley-Ryder.

Appearances: Ryan Szczepanik former Counsel for Joan Kelley-Ryder. David Gellman for Barbara McWhirter. Caroline Hinshaw Court appointed Counsel for Joan Kelley-Ryder (Conservatee). Amy Harrington for and with Franza Giffen (Trustee/Conservator). Frank Acuna appeared by telephone for Marianne Lindholm. Bonnie Ryder Bliss also present in Court.

The Parties did not object to Ms. Lindholm's petition. Mr. Szczepanik spoke of the issue of Ms. Kelley-Ryder's capacity, the amendments to the trust instruments with regard to the Successor Trustee provisions, the process of execution of said trust documents, events that occurred during the process, Ms. Giffen's inquiries as to whether she was named as Successor Trustee, actions taken by Ms. Giffen to have herself named as Successor Trustee, other services he provided, Ms. Kelley-Ryder and Ms. Kelley-Ryder's Daughter being adamant about not wanting Ms. Giffen as Successor Trustee, etc. Ms. Harrington explained why Ms. Giffen was appointed as durable Power of Attorney, why Northern Trust was not willing to serve, the Attorney in Fact's authority to amend the Trust, why Ms. Giffen stepped in, Ms. Kelley-Ryder's medical/mental condition, Hartog's office not acting in Ms. Kelley-Ryders best interest, etc. Mr. Gellman spoke of the issue of Counsel's duty to look into capacity issues prior to providing services not after. Ms. Hinshaw stated that she joins with the Conservator's objections and expressed concern about the Conservatee's estate planning being in order. All petitions were taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Terminate Powers Of Trustee The Joan Kelley-Ryder Trust; And To Appoint Franza Gi
Off Calendar Jun-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Gellman and Paul Melbostad for and with Barbara McWhirter (appearing by Court Call). Tracy Regli for and with Marianne Lindholm. Dennis Vann for Franza Giffen.

Ms. Ms.Hinshaw confirmed that Barbara Simon is no longer interested in being involved in this case. The Court held discussions with regard to the fees and stated that Ms. Lindholm, Ms. Giffen and Mr. Hartog need to file a petition for fees and that they should all be heard at the same time. Ms. Hinshaw questioned if the Trust needed to be under court supervision and the Court agreed that from the time of appointment of Barbara McWhirter the Trust is under Court Supervision. The Court noted that it is uncertain as to which document is the operative document. Mr. Gellman reported that the trust states that bond is not required regardless of who the trustee is and requested that if the Court is inclined to order bond, then it should be enough to cover the cash in the Trust. Mr. Gellman also raised the issue of what happens to the Trust upon death of the Conservatee. The Petition for Order Terminating Powers of Trustee of the Joan Kelley-Ryder Trust, etc. was granted in part as to the appointment of Barbara McWhirter as successor Trustee (subject to receipt and review of the written order). The balance (accounting/fees) of this petition was continued to July 09, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of filing accounting petitions by Mr. Hartog, Ms. Giffen, and Ms. Lindholm. When the first petition is filed and the hearing date set, the subsequent petitions are to be specially set with the same hearing date upon approval of the Courtroom Clerk. The Court advised Mr. Vann that original bank statements are needed.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.
